Filter.description:
OData Version 4.0 filter string (limited functionality).
Supports only 'eq' operations ('gt', 'ge', 'lt', 'le' and 'in' are NOT supported).
Supports only 'and' conjunction ('or' and 'not' are NOT supported).
Supported filter fields are: scopeName, address, city, state, country, zipcode, collectionName

childCount.description: number of child of this scope

scopeType.description: type of the scope. (site, site_collection, device_collection, org)

allOf.description: List of hierarchy with their properties.

code.description: status code.

message.description: Descriptive text on the outcome of the operation.

LimitParam.description: Specifies the number of results to return. (Default: 100) Maximum limit per query is 100.

OffsetParam.description: Specifies the pagination offset for the page of resources.

PaginationResponseProperties.description: Fields appended to a list to facilitate paging.

count.description: The number of objects returned in the current list.

offset.description:
An offset which could be passed in the next query to fetch the next page.
Set to null when no more pages remain.

total.description: The total number of objects contained in the datastore.

Sort.description:
Specifies the sort order of the field followed by sort direction.
Supports ASC and DESC.
Supported filter fields are: scopeName, address, city, state, country, zipcode, longitude, latitude, deviceCount, collectionName
. Example: scopeName ASC

rawOffset.description: A timezone offset represents the difference in time between a specific timezone and Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), in milliseconds.

timezoneId.description: A timezone name is a standardized designation for a region of the Earth where the same standard time is used, often based on geopolitical boundaries or geographical features.

timezoneName.description: A timezone ID is a unique identifier used to represent a specific timezone, typically following the "continent/city" format, allowing for accurate timekeeping and coordination across different locations.

OAuth2.description: OAuth 2.0 authentication with client credentials flow. You can also use your own access token.

info.description: This is a release of the Aruba Network Configuration APIs.
info.title: Scope Management

get.description: This API will return a list of device-groups, based on the query parameters. Each device-group in the returned list includes details like - deviceCount, id, type, description, scopeId, scopeName.

parameters.description: Number of device-groups that need to be fetched. (Default: 100) Maximum limit per query is 100.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Offset from where the device-groups need to be fetched.
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: OData 4.0 filter supports filtering on fields `scopeName`.
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Sort by field. May be one of `scopeName`, `deviceCount`.
parameters.name: sort

200.description: Returns the device-groups list with its properties.

post.description:
This API will create a device group with the specified scope name and description (optional).

Important: This API is supported only in Central On-Prem and standalone Central accounts. If your Central account has both Classic Central & Central (i.e. if you see the Classic Central toggle in the UI), this API is not supported & will not work.

requestBody.description: The device-groups create body.

200.description: Device-group creation successful and returns the scopeId of the Device-group created.

put.description:
This API will update the device group name and description (optional) based on the `scopeId`.

Important: This API is supported only in Central On-Prem and standalone Central accounts. If your Central account has both Classic Central & Central (i.e. if you see the Classic Central toggle in the UI), this API is not supported & will not work.

requestBody.description: The device-groups update body.

200.description: Device-group update successful and returns the scopeId of the Device-group updated.

post.description:
This API will add devices to a device group. 
 - `desScopeId` -> ScopeId of the device group to which devices should be associated. 
 - `devices` -> Array of device serials that need to be associated. This can be a serial of a device or stackId.

Important: This API is supported only in Central On-Prem and standalone Central accounts. If your Central account has both Classic Central & Central (i.e. if you see the Classic Central toggle in the UI), this API is not supported & will not work.

desScopeId.description: Scope ID of the device-groups to which devices should be associated.

devices.description: An array of device serials that need to be associated.

requestBody.description: The request body for adding devices to a device group.

200.description: Add-devices-to-device-groups successful.

post.description:
This API creates device group with devices. 
- `scopeName`: Name of the device group to which devices should be added
- `description`: Optional description for the device group
- `devices`: List of device serial numbers to be added.

Important: This API is supported only in Central On-Prem and standalone Central accounts. If your Central account has both Classic Central & Central (i.e. if you see the Classic Central toggle in the UI), this API is not supported & will not work.

requestBody.description: Request to add devices to a device group using serials.

200.description: Devices added successfully.

post.description:
This API will remove devices from a device group.
 - `devices` -> Array of device serials that need to be disassociated. This can be a serial of a device or stackId.

Important: This API is supported only in Central On-Prem and standalone Central accounts. If your Central account has both Classic Central & Central (i.e. if you see the Classic Central toggle in the UI), this API is not supported & will not work.

devices.description: An array of device serials that need to be disassociated.

requestBody.description: The request body for removing devices from a device group.

200.description: Remove-devices-from-device-groups successful.

delete.description:
This API is used to delete one or more existing device groups using a bulk delete operation.

Important: This API is supported only in Central On-Prem and standalone Central accounts. If your Central account has both Classic Central & Central (i.e. if you see the Classic Central toggle in the UI), this API is not supported & will not work.

items.description: An array of device group IDs to delete.

requestBody.description: The request body for bulk deleting device groups.

code.description: Response code.

items.description: List of deleted device group IDs.

message.description: Response message.

get.description: This API is used to fetch the Global Scope ID.

scopeId.description: The ScopeId of the global scope

200.description: Returns the global Scope ID of the customer

get.description: Returns the hierarchy of the given scopeId and its scopeType.

parameters.description: `scopeId` of resource site, site-collection, device-groups, org.
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: `type` may be one of scopeType (device, site, site-collection, device-groups, org).
parameters.name: type

200.description: Returns the hierarchy list with its scope IDs.

post.description:
This API will add devices to a site. 
 - `desScopeId` -> ScopeId of the site to which devices should be associated.
 - `devices` -> Array of device serials that need to be associated. This can be a serial of a device or stackId.

Important: This API is supported only in Central On-Prem and standalone Central accounts. If your Central account has both Classic Central & Central (i.e. if you see the Classic Central toggle in the UI), this API is not supported & will not work.

desScopeId.description: ScopeId of the site to which devices should be associated.

devices.description: An array of device serials that need to be associated. This can be a serial of a device or stackId.

requestBody.description: The request body for adding devices to a site.

200.description: Add-devices-to-site successful.

post.description: This API is used to add one or more available sites to existing site-collection.

requestBody.description: Add site to site-collection using scope ID. The site scope ID must exist in the system.

200.description: Returns a successful site association to the site-collection with the `scopeId` of sites which were associated successfully.

delete.description: This API is used to remove one or more available sites from the existing site collection.

parameters.description: Scope IDs of sites to be removed from site-collections.
parameters.name: site-id

202.description: Returns the list of `scopeId` of Sites which were removed from the site-collection successfully.

get.description: This API is used to fetch information about all site collections, based on the query parameters provided. Each site collection in the returned list includes - scopeId, scopeName, description, siteCount, deviceCount.

parameters.description: Number of site-collections that need to be fetched. (Default: 100) Maximum limit per query is 100.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Offset from where the site-collections need to be fetched.
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: OData 4.0 filter supports filtering on fields `scopeName`, `description`.
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Sort by field. May be one of `scopeName`, `description`, `deviceCount`, `siteCount`.
parameters.name: sort

200.description: Returns the site-collection list with its properties.

post.description: This API will create site collection with or without sites associated. To create empty site-collection (without any site associated) do not pass `siteIds` list.

requestBody.description: The site-collection create body.

200.description: Site-collection creation successful and returns the scopeId of the Site-collection created.

put.description: This API will update the site collection name and description based on the `scopeId`.

requestBody.description: The site-collection update body.

200.description: Site-collection update successful and returns the scopeId of the Site-collection updated.

delete.description: This API is used to delete one or more existing site collections using a bulk delete operation.

items.description: An array of site collection IDs to delete.

id.description: The site collection ID to delete.

requestBody.description: The request body for bulk deleting site collections.

code.description: Response code.

items.description: List of deleted site collection IDs.

message.description: Response message.

get.description: This API is used to fetch information about all sites, based on the query parameters provided. Each site in the returned list includes details like the scopeId, scopeName, collectionId, collectionName, deviceCount, image, the address params and geolocation.

parameters.description: Number of sites that need to be fetched. (Default: 100) Maximum limit per query is 100.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Offset from where the sites need to be fetched.
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: OData 4.0 filter supports filtering on fields `scopeName`, `address`, `city`, `state`, `country`, `zipcode`, `collectionName`.
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Sort by field. May be one of `scopeName`, `address`, `state`, `country`, `city`, `deviceCount`, `collectionName`, `zipcode`, `timezone`, `longitude`, `latitude`.
parameters.name: sort

200.description: Returns the site list with its properties.

post.description:
This API is used to create a site with address, geo coordinates (optional) and image (optional). Image needs to be uploaded first before referencing it here. 

 `timezone` : Timezone response from Google API for the corresponding geo coordinate.
 - `rawOffset` -> offset value from UTC (in seconds) for the given location from Google Timezone API.
 - `timezoneId` -> Timezone ID from the Google Maps API response
 - `timezoneName` -> long form name for the timezone from Google Maps API response.

requestBody.description: The site create body.

200.description: Site creation successful and returns the scopeId of the site created.

put.description:
This API is used to update name, address, geo coordinates or image for existing site based on its scopeId.
Image needs to be uploaded first before referencing it here. 

 `timezone` : Timezone response from Google API for the corresponding geo coordinate.
 - `rawOffset` -> offset value from UTC (in seconds) for the given location from Google Timezone API.
 - `timezoneId` -> Timezone ID from the Google Maps API response
 - `timezoneName` -> long form name for the timezone from Google Maps API response.

requestBody.description: The site update body.

200.description: Site update successful and returns the scopeId of the site updated.

delete.description: This API is used to delete one or more existing sites using a bulk delete operation.

items.description: An array of site IDs to delete.

requestBody.description: The request body for bulk deleting sites.

code.description: Response code.

items.description: The list of deleted site IDs.

message.description: Response message.

202.description: Site deleted successfully and returns the scopeId of the site deleted.

post.description: (Deprecated) This API will add devices to a device collection. Use /network-config/v1alpha1/device-groups-add-devices instead.

desScopeId.description: ScopeId of the device-collection to which devices should be associated

devices.description: Array of device serials that need to be associated

requestBody.description: Request body for adding devices to a device collection

200.description: add-devices-to-device-collection successful

post.description: (Deprecated) This API creates device collection with devices. Use /network-config/v1alpha1/device-groups-create-and-add-devices instead.

200.description: Devices added successfully

post.description: (Deprecated) This API will remove devices from a device collection. Use /network-config/v1alpha1/device-groups-remove-devices instead.

devices.description: Array of device serials that need to be disassociated

requestBody.description: Request body for removing devices from a device collection

200.description: remove-devices-from-device-collection successful

delete.description: (Deprecated) This API deletes one or more existing device collections. Send the scope IDs in a JSON list in the request body. Use /network-config/v1alpha1/device-groups/bulk instead.

scopeIds.description: List of scope IDs to delete

202.description: Device-Collection deleted successfully and returns the ScopeId of the deleted device collections

Important: This API is supported only in Central On-Prem and standalone Central accounts. If your Central account has both Classic Central & Central (i.e. if you see the Classic Central toggle in the UI), this API is not supported & will not work.

put.description:
(Deprecated) This API will update the device group name and description (optional) based on the `scopeId`. Use /network-config/v1/device-groups instead. 

Important: This API is supported only in Central On-Prem and standalone Central accounts. If your Central account has both Classic Central & Central (i.e. if you see the Classic Central toggle in the UI), this API is not supported & will not work.

 - `rawOffset` -> offset value from UTC (in seconds) for the given location from Google Timezone API.

 - `rawOffset` -> offset value from UTC (in seconds) for the given location from Google Timezone API.
 - `timezoneId` -> Timezone ID from the Google Maps API response
 - `timezoneName` -> long form name for the timezone from Google Maps API response. Use /network-config/v1/sites instead.

requestBody.description: site update body

200.description: Site update successful and returns the scopeId of the site updated.

delete.description: (Deprecated) This API is used to delete one or more existing sites using a bulk delete operation. Use /network-config/v1/sites/bulk instead.

items.description: Array of site IDs to delete

requestBody.description: Request body for bulk deleting sites

code.description: Response code

message.description: Response message

202.description: Site deleted successfully and returns the scopeId of the site deleted.
